# UC-13.1.52 · Prometheus Scrape Duration Seconds P99 Regression

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Availability &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch how long work takes across services so slowdowns in our code or a dependency show up while they are still small.*

---

## Description

Slow scrapes delay evaluations and can skip intervals on large targets.

## Value

Detects targets approaching scrape_timeout before silent metric gaps appear.

## Implementation

Ensure `job` and `instance` labels preserved; align `span` with scrape interval.

## SPL

```spl
| mstats avg(_value) AS avg_scrape WHERE index=prometheus metric_name="scrape_duration_seconds" span=5m BY job, instance
| where avg_scrape > 8
| sort - avg_scrape
```

## Visualization

Line chart (p99 scrape duration), Table (worst targets).

## Known False Positives

Latency spikes during pool exhaustion, garbage collection, or upstream slowness. We compare with the APM UI and deploy history before a code-only theory.

## References

- [Prometheus documentation — monitoring Prometheus](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/troubleshooting/)
- [Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/gdi/opentelemetry/opentelemetry.html)
